runs.summary: Memory index updated — folded in today's portfolio review/refresh plus several misplaced log entries from 2026-07-16/17/18 that a log-ordering quirk had hidden from the previous run
content: Domain Content Status — Finance: added 2026-07-18 trading-portfolio review (APH flagged Exit for the first time — RSI 38.6, below SMA50, MACD bearish, deferred to Monday; new candidates NEE/AXP/BMY) and portfolio-refresh (25 prices, IBM −26.5%); also backfilled the 2026-07-16 (VRTX/FHN) and 2026-07-17 (JBHT/ABT, 3 stop-outs) paper-trading runs that were missing from the prior update

Domain Content Status — Meta: updated wiki-thinker run count 11→14, added highlights for the 2026-07-16, -17, and -18 runs (Chip War/Micron bridge; Lightfin AI-animation question already answered; ABT trade vs. Health biomarker-tracking gap)
Domain Content Status — Podcasts: added the 2026-07-16 podcast-recommender run (4 shows); Cooking: updated recipe-recommender's 'latest run' pointer from a stale 2026-07-03 reference to 2026-07-17
Open Threads: added the APH first-Exit-signal item (tied to the existing AI-capex bear-case thread) and the new NEE/AXP/BMY investment-opportunity candidates

Data-quality note for future runs: log.md is not consistently newest-first — several scheduled tasks (daily-paper-trader, wiki-thinker) append to the true end of the file rather than prepending to the top, so entries from the last few days can be physically located near the bottom of a 600+ line file. Confirmed this by grepping full-file for `## \[2026-07-1[3-8]\]` rather than trusting a top-of-file read alone — worth doing this each run until the underlying logging behavior is unified
Appended one memory-update log line to log.md for 2026-07-18, placed at the top above today's other entries to match the file's dominant (if inconsistently applied) newest-first convention

Flagged a new operational item in Open Threads: this run's git commit (42e39a9) to fred-dashboard succeeded locally but the push was blocked by the auto-mode permission classifier (unattended, no user present) — the briefing is committed but not yet published; needs a manual `git push`

No new wiki pages found in index.md or log.md since the last update — no wiki-thinker, book/podcast recommender, or paper-trading run occurred today, so Domain Content Status table left unchanged this run
